01 / 07
Problem

Scaling support has always been
a human problem.

Humans are the bottleneckTriage and resolution depend on someone being available
Slow resolution timeContext lost and knowledge hunted each time
Volume spikes = headcount spikesYou can't handle 10× tickets without 10× people
02 / 07
How it works

Our agent reads, coordinates,
and acts — over email.

R
Rules — Plain-language policies: what the inbox handles, what it escalates.
T
Tools — Connected actions: check billing status, create Linear cards, query order history.
P
Permissions — Scoped autonomy: the agent acts within defined bounds.
A
Auditable — All traces saved in email inbox threads.
03 / 07
Why Now

Agents are ready. The operational layer is the gap.

🧠
LLMs can finally do the workWith tool use and MCP, agents resolve real tasks — not just chat. The bottleneck is now coordination, not capability.
✉️
Email is the agent protocolAsync, addressable, auditable, and universal — every system already speaks it. The natural substrate for agents to coordinate.
🔒
Autonomy needs guardrailsPermissions, approvals, and privacy are now table stakes. A fleet that acts on your behalf has to be governed and on-infra.
04 / 07
Technology

Built on production-grade infrastructure.

OpenAI Privacy FilterPII detection and redaction before any data leaves the system.
LangfusePrompt management and tracing — full observability over every agent decision.
AWS S3Scalable object storage for ticket attachments, audit logs, and thread history.
